title: Today's Tech Brief: Deus ex machina: Half of US Christians trust AI's spiritual advice
summary: A look at the intersection of technology and spirituality, with a focus on AI's role in religious guidance and Big Tech's antitrust landscape.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Deus ex machina: Half of US Christians trust AI's spiritual advice
imageCaption: Deus ex machina: Half of US Christians trust AI's spiritual advice
imageSourceTitle: Deus ex machina: Half of US Christians trust AI's spiritual advice

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. Let's dive into some interesting developments in the world of tech. First up, it seems that half of US Christians are open to receiving spiritual advice from AI. Yes, you read that right - AI preaching in a digital church. According to a report from The Register, this trend raises questions about the intersection of technology and spirituality. I mean, who needs a priest when you have a chatbot, right?

The idea that AI can provide spiritual guidance may seem far-fetched, but it's gaining traction. This development highlights the evolving role of AI in our lives, from simple tasks to more complex and personal areas. It's a fascinating example of how technology is changing the way we approach faith and spirituality. I'm curious to see how this trend will unfold and what it means for traditional religious practices.

On the regulatory front, Microsoft is reportedly under investigation by the FTC, potentially making it the next Big Tech target in antitrust actions. This comes 25 years after the company faced similar scrutiny. The Verge reports that this investigation could put Microsoft back in the hot seat, with implications for its cloud and AI strategies. It's a significant development that could shape the future of the tech industry.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Deus ex machina: Half of US Christians trust AI's spiritual advice
Microsoft could be the next Big Tech antitrust target
